title: Custom Item Models
description: Custom Item Models Mod powered by Custom Model Data Component
body:
# Custom Item Models

## Setup

This modification is only needed on the server side - clients without this mod will be able to join and use all provided functionality. It also works in singleplayer if installed on client.

## Usage

To see compatible ResourcePack examples, navigate into [tutorial](https://github.com/JustS-js/CIM/tree/1.21/tutorial) directory.

Rename Name Tag on the Anvil into the `string` that you would usually put inside `minecraft:custom_model_data={strings:[]}` array.

![img](https://github.com/JustS-js/CIM/blob/1.21/tutorial/rename_ruby.png?raw=true)
Diamond before applying Name Tag with the title `ruby`:
```
{
    count: 1, 
    Slot: 0b, 
    id: "minecraft:diamond"
}
```

Then, apply this Name Tag onto the item via Smithing Table.

![img](https://github.com/JustS-js/CIM/blob/1.21/tutorial/apply_ruby.png?raw=true)
Diamond after applying Name Tag with the title `ruby`:
```
{
    count: 1, 
    Slot: 0b, 
    id: "minecraft:diamond", 
    components: {
        "minecraft:custom_model_data": {
            strings: ["ruby"]
        }
    }
}
```

This mod also changes `minecraft:equippable` component for such items as armor pieces and elytras.

for example, `Diamond Helmet` before applying Name Tag with the title `crown`:
```
{
    count: 1, 
    Slot: 0b, 
    id: "minecraft:diamond_helmet"
}
```
![img](https://github.com/JustS-js/CIM/blob/1.21/tutorial/rename_crown.png?raw=true)
and after applying Name Tag with the title `crown`:
```
{
    count: 1, 
    Slot: 0b, 
    id: "minecraft:diamond_helmet", 
    components: {
        "minecraft:custom_model_data": {
            strings: ["crown"]
        }, 
        "minecraft:equippable": {
            equip_sound: "minecraft:item.armor.equip_diamond", 
            slot: "head", 
            asset_id: "cim:crown"
        }
    }
}
```
![img](https://github.com/JustS-js/CIM/blob/1.21/tutorial/apply_crown.png?raw=true)

ATTENTION: Be aware that `asset_id` **will always** be under `cim` namespace, due to how vanilla code operates. You can see examples in the [tutorial](https://github.com/JustS-js/CIM/tree/1.21/tutorial) directory.

*You don't have to use `cim` namespace for item models, only for armor rendering.*

![img](https://github.com/JustS-js/CIM/blob/1.21/tutorial/result.png?raw=true)
